meta.title: Fire detection and fire alarm systems — European product standard
meta.scope: Product certification requirements for all fire detection and alarm system components
meta.notes: EN 54 is a PRODUCT standard — it specifies how devices must perform. BS 5839 and NFPA 72 specify how to DESIGN with them.
meta.mandatory_in: All EU/EEA countries, widely referenced in Middle East, Africa, and Asia

parts.title: Introduction
parts.scope: Overview of the EN 54 series structure and general requirements

parts.title: Control and indicating equipment (CIE)
parts.scope: Requirements for fire alarm control panels
key_requirements: Display and indicate fire, fault, and disable conditions
Accept signals from detectors and manual call points

parts.title: Power supply equipment
parts.scope: Requirements for power supply units and battery chargers

parts.title: Heat detectors — Point heat detectors
parts.scope: Classification and testing of point-type heat detectors

classes.notes: Rate-of-rise only, no fixed temperature element

parts.title: Smoke detectors — Point smoke detectors using scattered light, transmitted light or ionization
parts.scope: Classification and testing of point-type smoke detectors

classes.notes: Most sensitive — for high-ceiling or critical applications

key_requirements: Response to standard fire test fires (TF1-TF8)
Immunity to false alarm stimuli (steam, dust, insects)
parts.notes: Class A1 and A2 detectors may be used at heights up to 12.5m per BS 5839-1 (Table 1)

parts.title: Flame detectors — Point flame detectors
parts.scope: Testing of flame detectors using infrared, ultraviolet, or multi-spectrum technology

types.use: General industrial, best false alarm rejection
Large open areas, tunnels, aircraft hangars

key_requirements: Response to test fires at various distances

parts.title: Manual call points
parts.scope: Requirements for manual fire alarm activation devices (pull stations / break-glass)
key_requirements: Operating force: ≤ 100N for resettable, ≤ 15N for break-glass element
Resettable elements preferred (frangible glass is being phased out)
IP rating minimum IP43 for indoor

parts.title: System performance assessment of components
parts.notes: Requires combined testing of panel + detectors + sounders as a system, not just individual components

parts.title: Guidelines for planning, design, installation, commissioning, use and maintenance
parts.scope: Design and installation guidelines (complementary to BS 5839)
parts.notes: European Technical Specification (CEN/TS) — provides guidelines for all EU countries

parts.title: Short-circuit isolators
key_requirements: Allow remaining circuit to operate normally

parts.title: Input/output devices
parts.scope: Requirements for interface modules (monitored input modules, relay output modules)

types.function: Control external device via relay output

parts.title: Aspirating smoke detectors
parts.scope: Requirements for aspirating (air sampling) smoke detection systems

classes.notes: Highest sensitivity — data centers, clean rooms

parts.notes: Multi-channel systems can have different sensitivity classes per pipe/channel

parts.title: Routing equipment
parts.scope: Requirements for fire alarm message routers and transmission equipment
parts.use: Network communication between panels and monitoring stations

parts.title: Fire alarm devices — Visual alarm devices
Components using radio links
parts.scope: Requirements for wireless fire alarm devices

parts.title: Multi-sensor fire detectors — Multi-sensor detectors
parts.scope: Requirements for detectors combining multiple sensing technologies (smoke + heat + CO)
parts.notes: Multi-criteria detectors are the fastest growing segment — better false alarm rejection

parts.title: Multi-sensor fire detectors — Point fire detectors with CO sensor
parts.scope: Requirements for smoke + CO combination detectors
parts.notes: CO sensing helps distinguish between smoldering fires and nuisance sources

fire_test_standards.description: EN 54 requires detectors to be tested against standardized fire test scenarios

test_fires.name: Wood fire (smoldering)
test_fires.notes: Smoldering fire test — smoke before flame

test_fires.name: Wood fire (flaming)
test_fires.material: European beech wood sticks, additional fuel

test_fires.name: N-heptane fire
test_fires.notes: Liquid fuel fire — high heat, moderate smoke

test_fires.name: Methylated spirit fire
test_fires.notes: Low smoke, high heat — tests heat detectors

test_fires.name: Hidden fire
test_fires.notes: Tests detector response to indirect smoke
